_description: Codex native surface × maturity × MeowKit policy. Each row is a verified claim about a Codex surface with its source and verification date. Authored from official docs (developers.openai.com/codex → learn.chatgpt.com/docs) and the codex-cli changelog; cross-checked against the local codex-cli 0.144.6 install.

surfaces.meowkitPolicy: primary — command handlers only; deny-capable safety hooks migrate as exit-2 wrappers

claims.claim: 10 hook events: SessionStart, SubagentStart, UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PermissionRequest, PreCompact, PostCompact, SubagentStop, Stop
Hooks enabled by default (no [features] flag) and support inline config.toml since Codex 0.124.0 stable
SessionStart matchers: startup, resume, clear, compact
PreToolUse/PostToolUse/PermissionRequest tool matchers include Bash, apply_patch, Edit, Write, update_plan, and the mcp__<server>__<tool> pattern (e.g. mcp__filesystem__read_file)
Agent-spawn matchers named Agent and spawn_agent
Only type:"command" handlers execute today; prompt and agent handler types are parsed but skipped
Non-managed (project-local) command hooks require explicit per-hook-definition user review/trust before running; managed hooks (system/MDM/requirements.toml) bypass

Schema: JSON on stdin (session_id, cwd, hook_event_name, tool_name, ...); exit 0 = success, exit 2 = block (stderr = reason); output supports hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext and permissionDecision
Claude-Code hook parity is a design similarity, not an OpenAI-documented compatibility promise (open GitHub issue #19385)

surfaces.meowkitPolicy: primary — reusable workflows; mk- prefix + unique names; pack-organized; default core pack within discovery budget

claims.claim: Location .agents/skills/<name>/SKILL.md, discovered walking CWD → repo root
Discovery budget: at most 2% of the model context window, or 8,000 characters when the window is unknown
Duplicate skill names are NOT merged — both appear in skill selectors
agents/openai.yaml is optional: UI metadata, allow_implicit_invocation (default true), MCP/tool dependency declarations

surfaces.meowkitPolicy: primary — short behavioral invariants only; no workflow dumps (those become skills)

claims.claim: Hierarchy global (~/.codex/AGENTS.override.md then ~/.codex/AGENTS.md) then project (git root → CWD, AGENTS.override.md then AGENTS.md at each level); concatenated root-down, closer-to-CWD = higher precedence
Default budget project_doc_max_bytes = 32 KiB (config.toml); once combined size hits limit, further files are silently truncated

surfaces.meowkitPolicy: primary — .codex/agents/*.toml auto-loaded; name field authoritative; roster re-authored by execution envelope

claims.claim: .codex/agents/*.toml (project) or ~/.codex/agents/*.toml (personal) auto-loaded; no config.toml wiring needed
Agent identified by its name field (source of truth); matching filename is a convention, not a requirement
Required fields name, description, developer_instructions; optional model, model_reasoning_effort, sandbox_mode, mcp_servers, skills.config
Model inheritance: explicit spawn value → agent-file default → [agents] config default → parent session value

surfaces.meowkitPolicy: primary — project .codex/config.toml; MCP servers via env-secret refs only; NO legacy [profiles.x] sections

claims.claim: Project override via .codex/config.toml, loaded walking project-root → CWD, closest file wins
Legacy [profiles.profile-name] TOML sections deprecated since 0.134.0 — replaced by standalone ~/.codex/<name>.config.toml files

--strict-config flag exists (Stable): errors when config.toml contains fields this Codex version does not recognize; supported by codex, exec, review, resume, fork, app-server, mcp-server, exec-server

surfaces.meowkitPolicy: optional/experimental — capability+version-gated with fallback; never sole enforcement (defense-in-depth only)

claims.claim: Rules are experimental and may change
Location ~/.codex/rules/*.rules (Starlark), prefix_rule(pattern, decision="allow"), most-restrictive-wins (forbidden > prompt > allow); no filesystem side effects by design
codex execpolicy check tests a rule against a command

surfaces.meowkitPolicy: omitted — no dual-write; .meowkit/memory stays separate; $CODEX_HOME/memories not synced

claims.claim: Stored under $CODEX_HOME/memories/ (default ~/.codex/memories/); base memories are preview, several sub-features (memory/reset, paginated thread history) explicitly experimental; Chronicle experimental (macOS + ChatGPT Pro only)

surfaces.meowkitPolicy: primary — STDIO + Streamable HTTP; secrets referenced via env vars, never inlined

claims.claim: Two server types: STDIO (command + args + env map) and Streamable HTTP (url + bearer_token_env_var); secrets are env-var-referenced

surfaces.meowkitPolicy: omitted — deprecated; skills are the documented successor

claims.claim: Custom prompts are deprecated; use skills for reusable instructions Codex can invoke explicitly or implicitly

openQuestions: Per-feature maturity table on the feature-maturity page could not be extracted via automated fetch (tool summarized away the table body); maturity labels above are inferred from individual feature pages + changelog, not a single authoritative table row.
Exact hook-event surface present at 0.124.0 vs 0.145.0 not separately verified; the live capability table treats any supported version as the full verified surface.
